items.title: FCC Chairman Brendan Carr’s war on the First Amendment

But since Donald Trump was elected to his second term, Carr has wielded that power to threaten broadcasters that have exercised their free speech rights to make jokes about...

items.title: Claude’s voice mode is now available for Opus and Sonnet
items.description: Until now, voice mode has only been available on Claude Haiku, Anthropic's faster but less powerful model. Now the company is making its Opus and Sonnet models available in voice mode, and extending its reach into apps like Gmail, Slack, and Canva. When Anthropic launched voice mode last year, it wa...

items.title: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of workers
items.description: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of its workers, or around 93 employees, as reported earlier by 404 Media. In a memo to employees, Patreon CEO Jack Conte writes that the company isn't making these changes "because we believe AI replaces humans," but says AI has "fundamentally transformed the tech in...

items.title: Microsoft is testing free, ad-supported cloud gaming for Xbox Insiders
items.description: Xbox Insiders can stream games from their libraries for free in ad-supported streaming sessions starting today. The free streaming sessions are limited to games you already own, and sessions are capped at one hour. However, ads will only play "before sessions begin." It's also optional - you won't n...

items.title: Amazon puts Luna cloud-streamed games like Fallout 4 inside Prime Video

items.title: Geekbench 7 will push your computer or phone even harder for better benchmarking
items.description: Primate Labs is releasing Geekbench 7, the latest generation of its popular benchmarking tool. Geekbench 7 features new video and audio encoding / decoding tests, a redesigned multi-core test, and larger, more demanding datasets for CPUs and GPUs to crunch through.
